ArchitecturalLevels.description: Shared order-processing rules imported by the documentation demo.

Layer.name: Presentation
Layer.description: Public entry points that translate requests into application actions.

Class.description: Endpoint suffixes are treated as public request handlers.
Types marked with ApiController also join the Presentation layer.

Layer.name: Application
Layer.description: Application orchestration that prepares orders without touching storage internals directly.

Class.description: Manager classes are accepted while older application code is being renamed.
The base class matcher catches application services even when their suffix changes.
Use-case implementations belong to the application layer.
Private nested helpers can be documented with access-modifier matching without broadening public layer ownership.

Layer.name: Persistence
Layer.description: Persistence entry points owned by the application, including repository namespaces.

Namespace.description: Types in the persistence namespace are repositories or repository helpers.

Layer.name: QuerySurface
Layer.description: Repository-owned fluent query builders that must be projected before leaving repository-owned code.

Class.description: Query objects are transient access points, not application dependencies.

Layer.name: Projection
Layer.description: Projected DTOs that are safe to return after query surfaces have done their work.

Class.description: Projection classes carry data outward without exposing query mechanics.

AllowedDependency.description: Presentation types may call the application layer to fulfill requests.
Presentation types may return projected DTOs, but should not depend on query surfaces or repositories.
Application code may use repositories, but should not retain them as mutable state in this example.
Application services may return projected DTOs after repository query surfaces have finished their work.
Repositories may create and return query surfaces as fluent access points.
Query surfaces may create projections and return only those projected objects.
